objectives: Complete security audit of k2payments ISO 20022 payments runtime
Review all 50+ Rust source files across 10+ crates
Report findings by severity: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, POSITIVES
constraints: Financial-grade system -- higher bar for crypto, auth, transport security
No unsafe code allowed per project CLAUDE.md
important_facts: Zero unsafe code blocks found in entire codebase
All SQL queries use parameterized bindings (sqlx)
AES-256-GCM encryption with SHA-256 as KDF (weak, no salt/iterations)
Legacy bearer auth accepts ANY non-empty token
TCP channel has unbounded frame allocation up to 4GB
Database URLs leaked in health/status API responses
No TLS on any listener (HTTP, gRPC, TCP)
Internal error messages forwarded to HTTP clients
No HTTP body size limits configured
mTLS subject header spoofable without proxy enforcement
JWT secret stored in plaintext TOML config
RocksDB uses full table scans for all queries
decisions_made: Classified TCP OOM as CRITICAL: single-line exploit for process crash
Classified SHA-256 KDF as HIGH: inadequate for financial-grade key derivation
Classified legacy bearer as HIGH: effectively no authentication
Classified DB URL leak as HIGH: credential exposure risk
risks_issues: C-1 TCP OOM: must fix before any network-facing deployment
H-1 KDF weakness: must fix before production
H-3 Legacy bearer: must remove or secure before production

iteration.summary: Completed full security audit of the k2payments ISO 20022 payments runtime. Read all 50+ Rust source files across all crates. Identified 1 CRITICAL (TCP unbounded frame allocation), 6 HIGH (weak KDF, Debug key leak, legacy bearer auth, DB URL exposure, no TLS, internal error forwarding), 6 MEDIUM (no body limits, no security headers, mTLS spoofing, plaintext JWT secret, RocksDB scans, no CORS), and 5 LOW findings. Also identified 10 positive security practices including zero unsafe code, parameterized SQL, sensitive field masking, and proper nonce generation. Delivered structured report to user.
inputs_consulted: Grep results for unsafe blocks, secret patterns, tracing statements
outputs_produced: Structured security audit report with CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW/POSITIVES sections
next_actions: User may request implementation of fixes for identified findings
Priority order: C-1 TCP frame limit, H-1 KDF, H-2 Debug leak, H-3 legacy bearer removal
